name: CHS 100 II
notes: Classic single-layer emulsion made from two emulsions; sensitization optimized for greyscale separation and large exposure latitude. Coated on clear archival PET (100 micron for 135 and 120; 175 micron for sheetfilm). Can be reversal processed. AHU plus backside AH/NC layer in 135 and 120; backside AH/NC layer only in sheetfilm. Made in Germany. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

name: CMS 20 II
notes: Ultra-high-resolution monodisperse emulsion resolving up to 800 lp/mm. Rated 20 ASA in dedicated ADOTECH developer (pictorial); up to 80 ASA for high contrast; 3-6 ASA in non-dedicated low-contrast developers. AHU anti-halation layer between emulsion and base; totally transparent base. Reciprocity: +1/2 stop at 1s and at 1/1000s. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

name: HR-50
notes: High-resolution film based on a technical emulsion; speed boosted to 50 ASA (ISO 50/17°) via ADOX SPEED BOOST technology, gamma reduced for pictorial use. Resolution up to 280 lp/mm at 1000:1 image contrast. Base 0.1mm PET; combined AHU/NC anti-halation. Usable as an infrared film and responds strongly to filtration. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

name: Scala 160
notes: Black and white reversal (slide) film designed specifically for reversal processing at 160 ASA; dedicated ADOX SCALA reversal kit available. Coated on 120 micron clear triacetate with increased silver content, yielding DMAX of 3.6 or higher. Anti-halation layer between emulsion and base. Not DX coded. When shot as a negative, expose at 80-100 ASA. Reciprocity: +1/2 from 1s, +1 from 10s, +2 from 100s. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.

name: Scala 50
notes: High-resolution B&W slide film based on a technical emulsion; speed boosted to 50 ASA (ISO 50/17°) with gamma reduced for pictorial use. Resolution up to 280 lp/mm at 1000:1 image contrast. Base 0.1mm PET; combined AHU/NC anti-halation. Processed as a slide in the dedicated ADOX SCALA reversal kit; can also be processed as a negative using HR-50 times. Usable as an infrared film. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.

name: Silvermax 100
notes: Currently discontinued (ADOX recommends CHS 100 II / Scala 50 as alternatives). APX 100 recipe with finer grain, slightly lower speed and ~30% higher silver content. Achieves up to 14 stops copy range in dedicated Silvermax developer. Coated on 120 micron clear triacetate; anti-halation layer between emulsion and base; can optionally be reversal-processed to a B&W slide (ADOX Scala). Not DX coded. Reciprocity: +1/2 from 1s, +1 from 10s, +2 from 100s. Made in Germany. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

name: PAN-X Reverso Super 8
notes: True B&W Super 8 reversal film, projectable right after development; positioned as a replacement for the discontinued Kodak Plus-X. Currently unavailable. Silver-rich emulsion with full shadow detail at 100 ASA and DMAX above 3.0; anti-halation layer between base and emulsion; clear triacetate base. 15 meters (~3 min 20 s). Standard developing in Kodak D-94A process; expose at 64 or 100 ASA (best results at 64 ASA); cartridge notched 100 ASA Tungsten. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.
